Video.summary: The boys are on the Raven in Missouri trying to plant 50 acres of Big Tine food plots in 5 days before heading out west to kick off the hunting season.
Video.title: "50 Acres In 5 Days"

Video.summary: TA Mahoney is a classic mom and pop store known for going the extra mile and treating everyday people like family. Keep an eye on Mike though, you never know what kind of prank is up his sleeve.
Video.title: "Mike Mahoney"

Video.summary: We kick off Season 2 in Tennessee where we stop in Nashville, Townsend, and Johnson City!!
Video.title: "Traveling Over 2,000 Miles to Fish HERE!"

Video.summary: This episode we make our way through the mountains of western North Carolina, where we stop in Asheville and Cashiers!!
Video.title: "Dry Fly Fishing HEAVEN (Wild Trout)"

Video.summary: Josh and his wife Anna own the most prolific tackle shop in Fort Walton Beach. Josh's family eats, sleeps and breathes fishing.
Video.title: "Josh Calhoon"

Video.summary: Join us for an unforgettable Women Go Hunting experience at Legends Ranch in Michigan!
Video.title: "Women Go Hunting"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade and his son take on the turkey woods for an afternoon hunt. Although the woods remained quiet, a tom soon snuck out of the woods and on the end of Bentley's barrel! After that Slade was able to help a client of his harvest his first turkey! And boy was it an action packed hunt! Jakes, hens, and tons of gobbles, This hunt is spectacular! Lastly, Slade introduces a property that we have already made a video on, but with just how amazing the land is, we had to include it! Floweree is the epitome of a sportsman's paradise!
Video.title: "Two More Turkeys Down in Mississippi!!!"

Video.summary: Justin Webb heads to Tennessee to hunt with Phillip Culpepper, hoping to get an Eastern in his quest for a Grand Slam. Next stop - Wyoming for a Merriam.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Big Eastern Dreams"

Video.summary: Rusty Creasey joins the Spring Thunder crew for an incredible turkey hunt in Oklahoma. The guys are blessed to fill 6 tags in a short time and have several memorable hunts.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Oklahoma"

Video.summary: With Meredith McCord halfway around the world, Capt. Justin Leake gives show producer Chris Hermans his first shot at landing a tarpon after filming them for 9 seasons. Does art imitate life?
Video.title: "Tarpon Producer"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin Leake joins some old friends on board the Fall Line in hopes of scoring big in the Bay Point Billfish Open. This trip will test the limits of all the anglers as the skilled captain and crew do the impossible in hopes of bringing home the prize.
Video.title: "Billfish Tourney"

Video.summary: Florida WaterSports Jeff Lange & Capt Justin head out of PCB in the wee morning hours hoping to land a limit of tasty table fare before the window of opportunity closes. Pro tip: Electric reels save shoulders.
Video.title: "We’re gonna need a bigger cooler."

Video.summary: Each year we get to spend time in camp with some of our best friends. Zach from Big & J gets some time in a blind while reflecting on what hunting in Texas means to him. Texas provides a wide variety of game, and chasing these creatures all across the state is some of the best times had.
Video.title: "Valued Partners"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade takes us through his new investment property, and breaks down - what’s involved in taking a raw land tract and creating extremely desirable turn-key - hunting properties. - Caleb and Slade double up on some Wilkinson County gobblers for Calebs first turkey - kill ever.
Video.title: "Flips And Flops"

Video.summary: With Montana in the rearview mirror the Flylords team crosses over into Idaho. In this leg of the trip they hit Blue-Ribbon trout waters with the folks from Henrys Fork Anglers and Silver Creek Outfitters. They also have a chance to sit down with the owners of these operations and hear what these shops and fly fishing mean to them. Before they hit the road to Utah the crew joins in on another conservation event at Payette Brewing Company.
Video.title: "SIGHT Casting RISING Trout in IDAHO"

Video.summary: It’s the final leg of the Fly Shop Tour and what better way to end it than with a trip down to see the crew at Fly Fish Food. We learn the unique story of how owners Clark “Cheech” Pierce & Curtis Fry started their shop and spend some time chasing hungry Cutthroat. Wrapping it all up the team puts on the final conservation event of the season with Fly Fish Food and Fish For Garbage, as well as spending some time behind the oars on the Green River with crew at Western Rivers Fly Shop.
Video.title: "The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"

Image.alt: "The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"
"The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"
"The END of our 2500 Mile Road Trip"

Video.summary: Explore Pakistan's wilderness with Dan Cabela as he treks the rugged mountains, in search of ibex and urial, and learns about the community conservation model that has protected these species.
Video.title: "Community Conservation in Pakistan"

Video.summary: Discover the pressing threat of Chronic Wasting Disease in North American whitetail deer and the fierce debate around the strategies aimed at its management.
Video.title: "Battling Disease in Whitetail"

Video.summary: Randy is getting the last food plot of the season in down at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma. Then opening day of the 2025 bow season starts off in an unusual way for the crew. Randy smokes a Hog and Nate Has a really close call with one of the target bucks.
Video.title: "Bow Camp 2025 at The Dirty Bird"

Video.summary: From fuzzy velvet to hardened bone, antlers are more than headgear—they're nature’s calendar. In this episode of Deer & Deer Hunting, we explore the fascinating stages of whitetail antler development, what they reveal about a buck’s age and health, and how understanding this process can help you become a better hunter. Join us as we break down the biology, bust myths, and show real examples from the field.
Video.title: "Stages of Antler Development"

Video.summary: Mark is wrapping up his Argentina hunt and heading to Entre Rios to track down the final two birds on his list: the Rock Pigeon and the Eared Dove. This area is home to over 10 million birds, making it a prime hunting ground. With flocks of pigeons and doves filling the skies, Mark is set for a fast-paced, action-packed finish to his hunt—a perfect end to an unforgettable adventure.
Video.title: "South America Upland Slam - Entre Rios"

Video.summary: John Sewell has played a huge part in the development of feeding Fortified Cottonseed 365 days a year. Laramie Barrett of Fortified Cottonseed joins John for a great weekend of hunting at his ranch in Uvalde, Texas. This is a great time for Laramie to get away from the office and see the first hand of how their product is performing.
Video.title: "Fortified Cottonseed"

Video.summary: This week we are rolling along side the crew from Double T Outfitters. Management plays a huge role in every outfitters success every year. Bret and his team have a strong management plan in place and these guys had a blast with a group of out of state hunters.
Video.title: "Double T Outfitters"

Video.summary: Another great hunt this week with Intrepid Care. We have the chance to hang out with some Veterans from all over the country. We gather to tell stories, build relationships, and just enjoy the great outdoors. This trip is action packed and the guys are chasing South Texas hogs from the ground.
Video.title: "Intrepid Care"

Video.summary: Batter Batter Swing………. Well, you might see where this might be headed. But, Hey!! Slade is in the great state of Kansas. Its mid-October and hes looking to bring home Lori some extra home décor.
Video.title: "Buckin' Kansas"

Video.summary: A Southwest Mississippi GIANT “Barry” has Slade’s brain in scrambles as he is preparing to head to Iowa after waiting 5 years to finally draw his tag. The chase is on let’s see if the Hunting Land Man can seal a deal!
Video.title: "Iowa Madness"

Video.summary: A Southwest Mississippi GIANT “Barry” has made more daylight appearances than possibly any mature bucks in existence. Now that Slade is back from the midwest The chase is on.
Video.title: "Barry"

Video.summary: In this episode Slade gets very busy in the real estate world. But lucky for him, Caleb Armstrong takes up the slack. Caleb sets out to hunt swamp bucks on the public land of Tensas Parish.
Video.title: "Tensas Parish"

Video.summary: LBP Spotlights West End Boatworks and goes offshore fishing.
Video.title: "West End Boatworks"

Video.summary: LBP Spotlights Clay Shidler and the history of his charter business, Hang'Em High Sportfishing.
Video.title: "Clay Shidler"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair heads up to Steinhatchee after getting a call from local tournament angler Jay Andrew. The reds have been popping off and so the two head out in search.
Video.title: "Top Water Reds with Jay Andrew"

Video.summary: The second day in Steinhatchee, Capt. Blair & Jay finally find where all the reds got off too, and I mean ALL the reds. This is one of the craziest sights on the show yet.
Video.title: "The School"

Video.summary: Highlights from Capt. Blair's recent travels.
Video.title: "Bits & Bites"

Video.summary: While up in Alaska, the crew caught more fish than we had time to show so here's another look at some of the fish that were caught on the Alaskan trip.
Video.title: "Alaska Bits & Bites"

Video.summary: Kyle explores the Great Lakes on his Sea-Doo in search of walleye and lake trout.
Video.title: "Great Lakes Extreme Fishing"

Video.summary: Kyle Green and his buddy Jeremy set out on a buffalo hunt in the great state of Oklahoma. Along the way they learn about the true past and potential future of the American Buffalo from several interesting people with different perspectives.
Video.title: "Bison Bison Bison"

Video.summary: Kyle Green heads to Yankton, South Dakota to learn about the history of the Missouri river and the people who count on the river for sustenance. The invasive carp are set to destroy this rich ecosystem and Kyle aims to stop this fish with his best weapon, a great recipe.
Video.title: "Invasive Carp Disaster"

Video.summary: Kyle Green meets up with his friends at the National Wild Turkey Federation to dive into turkey conservation, and hunt the largest subspecies of wild turkey. Kyle finishes off this episode with his most favorite wild game recipe, boneless wild turkey wings.
Video.title: "The Great Eastern Turkey"

Video.summary: Tyler Jordan heads back to his college stomping grounds at Ole Miss to hunt with Athletic Director Keith Carter and William Jones. After a tough few days they were finally able to find some birds that were willing to work!
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Ole Miss Double"

Video.summary: Michael Pitts of Realtree Road Trips joins Anthony Virga and Matt Garger hunting the hills of New York.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Pitts Hunts New York"

Video.summary: Justin and Brandi with Big Dreams Outdoors finish up their slam hunting Merriam turkeys with 7J Outfitters in Wyoming. It was a tough week for Justin but persistence pays off!
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Big Merriam Dreams"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin and Florida WaterSports pro Jeff Lange go deep off Panama City Beach to test the limits of a new battery powered reel. The results are surprising.
Video.title: "Electric Deep Drop"

Video.summary: When tarpon don’t cooperate, it’s time for Capt. Justin and friend Ethan Register to have a competition for dinner options in the gulf’s grocery store.
Video.title: "Once Upon a Wahoo"

Video.summary: Highlighting guests Dalton Smith; Dalton coached Campbellsville University Kayak Bass Team. Fished high school in KY; Mark watched him as a youth angle. Dalton caught two 14lb+ bass on the same day once. The 23-year-old uprooted from his area to follow this dream full time in OH.
Video.title: "Bassin’ with Dalton Smith"

Video.summary: The Direction TV's Annual Military Appreciation Episode. Two good friends, Thomas & Terry McWilliams, help to make this event take place. This episode is dedicated to our service men and women.
Video.title: "Bassin’ with Americas Hero’s"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared joins his best new bud, Capt. Garrett George for a summertime Mahi bash.
Video.title: "Islamorada Mahi"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Florida Keys guide Jeff Malone to find a productive wreck with which they enjoy the exciting variety of fish that wrecks can provide.
Video.title: "Jeff Malones New Wreck"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by marine scientist Nick Castillo to search for bonefish in Key Largo for both fun and study.
Video.title: "Bonefish Science With Nick Castillo"

Video.summary: Every year, hunters from across the country flock to various parts of Texas to extend their hunting season. This year, our friends from Struttinbuck did just the same. Matt and Clede make the long drive to Del Rio to hunt South Texas bucks right on the Mexico border.
Video.title: "Border Bucks"

Video.summary: 2 down, 1 to go. Anthony Virga’s the next man up for a gobbler with Michael Pitts and Matt Garger in New York’s Adirondack Mountains.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Virga New York Kill"

Video.summary: Mark Peterson is in the final stretch of completing the South America Upland Slam. Having already checked off nine species in Argentina, he now finds himself in Peru, where he faces the challenge of tracking down two elusive species to complete his list: the Andean Tinamou and the Western Peruvian Dove.
Video.title: "South America Upland Slam - Peru"

Video.summary: Well-known for producing some of the highest quality firearms for more than a century, the Browning X-Bolt can lift the “average Joe” hunter to the level of dead-eye marksman. To showcase how simple it can be, self-proclaimed “average hunter” Brandon Bates takes a brand new Browning X-Bolt right out of the box and goes to the range to see what this production rifle can do in “custom rifle” scenarios.
Video.title: Rmef - "Out Of The Box"

Video.summary: Join Benny Spies of Team Winchester as he shares stories of his upbringing in South Dakota. From cherished family photos to duck seasons of the past, learn how a passion for hunting was ignited and became a way of life for Benny and his family.
Video.title: "The Spies Tradition"

Video.summary: Follow TTHA President/CEO Christina Pittman and her husband Matt this week as they chase Whitetails at Morrow Ranch. This is a hunt that they had purchased earlier at the Disabled Outdoorsman in the year to be able to spend some time in the woods. A few days away to relax and spend quality time with loved ones should always be a priority. Morrow Ranch is definitely a go to place for low fence Texas hunting.
Video.title: "Morrow Ranch"

Video.summary: This is the story of how FE Hill ranch got it's start. From its inception, FE Hill has created opportunities for people to experience the outdoors and create memories. For us, it has created a tradition of Fathers and Sons hunting together.
Video.title: "The Hill"

Video.summary: This week our team visits The Rocky Creek Ranch with two long time supporters of TTHA, Blake Barnett and John Fink. The goal in this episode is white management, which is something both Blake and John are passionate about. Blake has been hunting many years but what takes place in this hunt is a first.
Video.title: "A Perfect Six"

Video.summary: From time to time you have moments that will change the way think about something. That is what happened for us while hunting the Scimitar Oryx with Republic Outdoors. Hunting is more than harvesting animals, it’s conservation, and in this story, we learn how a species nearly extinct gets another chance to live.
Video.title: "A Story Of Restoration"
